dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6578 IH: Charlie Kirk Freedom of Speech Plaza Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Mace (for herself, Mr. Steube, and Mrs. Luna) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform

form.official-title: To designate the area of 16th Street Northwest between H Street Northwest and K Street Northwest in the District of Columbia as the Charlie Kirk Freedom of Speech Plaza, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Charlie Kirk Freedom of Speech Plaza Act.

section.header: Designation of Charlie Kirk Freedom of Speech Plaza

paragraph.text: The area of 16th Street Northwest between H Street Northwest and K Street Northwest in the District of Columbia (formerly symbolically designated as Black Lives Matter Plaza under the Black Lives Matter Plaza Designation Act of 2020 (sec. 9–204.01 note, DC Official Code)), shall be known and designated as Charlie Kirk Freedom of Speech Plaza.

Any reference in a law, map, regulation, document, paper, or other record of the United States to the area referred to in paragraph (1) shall be deemed to be a reference to Charlie Kirk Freedom of Speech Plaza.
The District of Columbia shall construct not less than 2 street signs that shall—

subparagraph.text: contain the phrase Charlie Kirk Freedom of Speech Plaza; and
be similar in design to the signs used by the District of Columbia to designate the location of Metro stations.

paragraph.text: Not later than 90 days after the date of the enactment of this Act, the District of Columbia shall place the signs constructed under paragraph (1) above existing signs at the intersection of 16th Street Northwest and H Street Northwest and the intersection of 16th Street Northwest and K Street Northwest.
